title: Exercises: Quantum Errors and Their Nature

exercises.title: No-Cloning Proof
exercises.description: Implement a computational proof of the no-cloning theorem.
tasks: Create a hypothetical cloning unitary for |0⟩ and |1⟩
Apply it to |+⟩ using linearity
Compare result with what true cloning would produce
Calculate the fidelity between the two outcomes
Demonstrate that perfect cloning is impossible
exercises.expectedOutput: Proof that cloning fidelity is less than 1

exercises.title: Pauli Error Decomposition
exercises.description: Decompose arbitrary single-qubit errors into the Pauli basis.
tasks: Implement general error decomposition function
Decompose rotation errors R_x(θ), R_y(θ), R_z(θ)
Decompose depolarizing channel Kraus operators
Verify reconstruction: E = Σᵢ eᵢPᵢ
Plot error probabilities vs rotation angle

exercises.title: Measurement Information Loss
exercises.description: Quantify information loss from direct measurement.
tasks: Create ensemble of states with same Z-measurement statistics
Calculate von Neumann entropy before measurement
Calculate classical entropy after measurement
Show that phase information is lost
Demonstrate that |+⟩, |-⟩, |+i⟩, |-i⟩ are indistinguishable
hints: Pure state has S(ρ) = 0 von Neumann entropy
Measurement outcome entropy: H = -Σp log p
Phase information contributes to quantum fidelity
These states form a sphere at z=0 on Bloch sphere

exercises.title: Error Discretization Simulator
exercises.description: Simulate how syndrome measurement discretizes continuous errors.
tasks: Create a continuous error (small rotation)
Apply to a 3-qubit bit-flip code state
Simulate syndrome measurement
Show probability of collapsing to each discrete error
Verify error correction succeeds with high probability
hints: Small rotation: R_x(θ) ≈ I + (iθ/2)X for small θ
Syndrome measurement projects onto error subspaces
Success probability ≈ cos²(θ/2) for single-qubit error
